finding: Pre-commit governance blocks can fail a legitimate framework-repo commit because `framework claim` / direction-lock state is missing, even when the diff content, validators, and historical governance intent are otherwise correct. The hook currently treats governance timing gaps as a hard commit failure instead of distinguishing them from content-integrity failures.
expectedBehavior: Framework-repo pre-commit should surface a precise deferred-governance diagnosis, and historical-batch or closeback flows should have an explicit, governed path to satisfy or defer framework claim / direction-lock requirements without forcing blanket `--no-verify`.
evidenceOrRepro: Closed on 2026-06-28 by teaching `packages/cli/src/commands/hook.ts` to classify pre-commit failures into governance-state versus content-validation lanes instead of flattening everything into one generic block. `failureEnvelope` now exposes `governanceStateFailures`, `contentValidationFailures`, and `deferredGovernanceCandidate`, while governance-only failures surface the dedicated hook code `ATM_HOOK_PRE_COMMIT_DEFERRED_GOVERNANCE_REQUIRED` so operators can tell that the staged content is not failing validation and the remaining blocker is claim/governance timing. Regression coverage was added to `scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts`, which now stages a framework-critical file in a fixture repo, forces validator success, and proves the hook reports the deferred-governance marker instead of a generic content-failure-only diagnosis.
followUp: Keep the deferred-governance split in `packages/cli/src/commands/hook.ts` and the fixture-backed regression in `scripts/validate-git-hooks-enforcement.ts`; future hook refactors must preserve a first-class governance-state diagnosis whenever the staged content is otherwise validator-clean.
